name: Desfiladero del Río Purón y Alto de Revillallanos - desde Herrán — circular por el Parque Natural de Valderejo
region_intro: Hiking around Valle De Tobalina, located in the northwest of Burgos, Spain, offers diverse natural landscapes for outdoor enthusiasts. The region is characterized by riverine environments, including the Ebro and Purón rivers, which have carved impressive gorges. It features mountainous terrain with peaks like Humion and Mancubo, alongside the Montes Obarenes - San Zadornil Natural Park, preserving its geological and biodiversity interests. Best hiking trails around Valle De Tobalina The most popular hiking route is Desfiladero del Río Purón y Alto de Revillallanos - desde Herrán — circular por el Parque Natural de Valderejo, a 9.9 miles (16.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 27 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Humion – Pico Humión loop from Embalse de Sobrón, a difficult 6.7 miles (10.8 km) path. This trail ascends towards Pico Humión, offering views of the surrounding mountainous landscape. Local hikers also love the Ribera – Purón River Gorge loop from Herrán, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail leading through the Purón River Gorge, often completed in about 2 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around Valle De Tobalina is defined by river gorges, mountainous terrain, and protected natural park areas. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate river loops to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Valle De Tobalina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 260 reviews. More than 1600 hikers have used komoot to explore Valle De Tobalina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find in Valle De Tobalina?
A: Valle De Tobalina offers a diverse range of hiking trails, from easy, accessible paths to challenging mountain ascents. You'll find routes through impressive river gorges like the Purón River Gorge, along the Ebro River, and up prominent peaks such as Humion and Mancubo. The region is also part of the Montes Obarenes - San Zadornil Natural Park, ensuring varied and preserved natural environments.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Valle De Tobalina?
A: Yes, Valle De Tobalina has 3 easy routes suitable for families or those seeking a more relaxed walk. These trails often follow riverine landscapes, offering gentle gradients and scenic views without significant elevation gain. For example, the area features moderate river loops that are less strenuous.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes in Valle De Tobalina?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers, Valle De Tobalina offers 3 difficult routes with significant elevation gains and varied terrain. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045956074/desfiladero-del-rio-puron-y-alto-de-revillallanos-desde-herran-circular-por-el-parque-natural-de-valderejo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero del Río Purón y Alto de Revillallanos - desde Herrán&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15000750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Humion – Pico Humión loop from Embalse de Sobrón&lt;/a&gt; provide strenuous options with rewarding views from peaks like Humion and Mancubo.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Valle De Tobalina?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Valle De Tobalina are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10508261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera – Purón River Gorge loop from Herrán&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through a picturesque gorge, or the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24636740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Humion – Mancubo loop from Embalse de Sobrón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking in Valle De Tobalina?
A: Valle De Tobalina is rich in natural beauty. You can explore impressive river gorges like the Purón River Gorge, witness the stunning 20-meter high Pedrosa de Tobalina waterfall on the Jerea River, or enjoy views from peaks such as Humion and Mancubo. The region is also part of the Montes Obarenes - San Zadornil Natural Park, offering diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural points of interest near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region's history adds a unique dimension to hiking. You might encounter medieval structures, such as those in Herrán with its traditional architecture. Ancient necropolises, like the one in Peña del Mazo near Pajares, are also present. Additionally, the nearby medieval town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6302190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frías&lt;/a&gt;, with its impressive bridge over the Ebro, is a notable cultural highlight often combined with visits to Valle De Tobalina. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4452287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de los Varona&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5702187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Gadea del Cid Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Valle De Tobalina?
A: The hiking routes in Valle De Tobalina are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 260 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the beauty of the river gorges, and the rewarding views from the mountain ascents.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to visit in Valle De Tobalina?
A: Yes, one of the most spectacular natural features is the Pedrosa de Tobalina waterfall on the Jerea River. This 20-meter high curtain of water forms a beautiful, crystal-clear pool, making it an ideal spot for a break or picnic during your hike.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Valle De Tobalina?
A: While the guide doesn't specify a single 'best' season, the diverse landscapes of Valle De Tobalina, including riverine areas and mountainous terrain, suggest that spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, especially in the gorges, while winter might offer unique, quieter experiences on some trails.

Q: Are there opportunities for long-distance hiking in the area?
A: Yes, the GR99, a long-distance path, traverses the Ebro's slopes within Valle De Tobalina. This offers options for those interested in multi-day treks or longer sections of a trail, catering to both hiking and mountain biking enthusiasts.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Valle De Tobalina?
A: There are 11 distinct hiking trails documented in Valle De Tobalina, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These include 3 easy, 5 moderate, and 3 difficult routes.

Q: What are the prominent mountain passes in Valle De Tobalina?
A: The region features several notable mountain passes that can be explored. These include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6048516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Horca Pass (902 m)&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5580127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo de Busto&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5117925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo Cantiportillo&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic viewpoints and connecting different parts of the mountainous terrain.
